$comment: Archetypal UAE cardholder profiles. Each is a monthly spend vector over the canonical categories plus behaviour. `international_share` is the fraction of TOTAL monthly spend that is non-AED. `revolves`=true means the customer carries a balance (pays interest); this flips which card is 'best' because APR then dwarfs cashback. These profiles are inputs to the value engine, and the web demo lets a user tune them.

profiles.label: Young Professional — Foodie
profiles.description: Single, eats out constantly, orders delivery, some travel. Pays the card in full every month.

profiles.label: Family — Groceries & School Fees
profiles.description: Dual-income family, big grocery basket, school fees, fuel, utilities. Disciplined: pays in full.

profiles.label: Frequent Traveler — Premium
profiles.description: High earner, flies monthly, big FX and travel spend, values lounges and travel insurance. Pays in full.

profiles.label: Digital Native — Online & Mobile Wallet
profiles.description: Younger user, shops online, taps Apple/Google Pay for almost everything, modest income. Pays in full.

profiles.label: Household — Bills & Utilities
profiles.description: Pays the household: DEWA, du/Etisalat, Salik, school fees. Moderate discretionary spend. Pays in full.

profiles.label: Revolver — Carries a Balance
profiles.description: Spends moderately but does NOT clear the statement; carries ~AED 9,000 month to month. For this person APR, not cashback, decides everything.

profiles.label: New to Credit — Low Income
profiles.description: First card, salary just above entry thresholds, needs no/low fee and an easy approval. Pays in full.

profiles.label: High Spender — Maximizer
profiles.description: Routes everything through one card, >AED 20k/month, optimizes hard. Pays in full. Unlocks top spend-tier bands.
